steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to describe the study's research methodology — how the study was set up and conducted, not its goals or its results. A methodology description covers the experimental design: what was tested and how groups were structured.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'tested 125 ingested seeds and 125 non-ingested seeds': Describes the experimental design — two groups of 125 seeds tested under different conditions. This is the methodology. ✓
  • Choice B — 'wanted to explore the effects of bat ingestion on germination': States the researchers' goal, not how they carried out the study. ✗
  • Choice C — 'a lower percentage of ingested seeds germinated': Reports a finding from the results, not the method used. ✗
  • Choice D — '82 of 125 ingested seeds germinated': States a specific result (66% germination rate), not the experimental structure. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
